key_insight: A frozen LLM plus task-isolating LoRA experts can learn motion understanding and motion generation sequentially with near-zero forgetting.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical continual-learning path for motion-language agents that must absorb new movement styles without degrading prior capabilities.
method: Freeze the backbone LLM and attach low-rank LoRA adapters for continual learning.
Use mixture-of-experts variants with an autoencoder-based router to pick experts at inference without task labels.
Compare hard expert selection versus soft expert blending across both M2T and T2M tasks.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates a reproducible five-task benchmark derived from HumanML3D via semantic clustering, reporting high captioning and generation quality with near-zero forgetting.
watch_item: Routing quality and task isolation are critical; soft expert blending can reduce performance, and token-level accuracy may not reflect downstream motion quality.
paper_title: Towards Continual Motion-Language Agents: LoRA Variants for Incremental Motion Understanding and Generation
